2603.26663.title: Weight Tying Biases Token Embeddings Towards the Output Space
2603.26663.paper_summary: Weight tying, i.e. sharing parameters between input and output embedding matrices, is common practice in language model design, yet its impact on the learned embedding space remains poorly understood. In this paper, we show that tied embedding matrices align more closely with output (unembedding) matrices than with input embeddings of comparable untied models, indicating that the shared matrix is shaped primarily for output prediction rather than input representation. This unembedding bias arises because output gradients dominate early in training. Using tuned lens analysis, we show this negatively affects early-layer computations, which contribute less effectively to the residual stream. Scaling input gradients during training reduces this bias, providing causal evidence for the role of gradient imbalance. This is mechanistic evidence that weight tying optimizes the embedding matrix for output prediction, compromising its role in input representation. These results help explain why weight tying can harm performance at scale and have implications for training smaller LLMs, where the embedding matrix contributes substantially to total parameter count.
2603.26663.paper_summary_zh: 權重綁定，即在輸入和輸出嵌入矩陣之間共享參數，是語言模型設計中的常見做法，但其對學習到的嵌入空間的影響仍然不甚了解。<lb/>在本文中，我們展示了綁定的嵌入矩陣與輸出（去嵌入）矩陣之間的對齊程度比與可比的未綁定模型的輸入嵌入更為緊密，這表明共享矩陣主要是為了輸出預測而塑造的，而非輸入表示。<lb/>這種去嵌入偏差的產生是因為在訓練初期，輸出梯度佔主導地位。<lb/>通過調整透鏡分析，我們顯示這對早期層的計算產生負面影響，這些計算對殘差流的貢獻效果較差。<lb/>在訓練期間縮放輸入梯度可以減少這種偏差，提供了梯度不平衡作用的因果證據。<lb/>這是機制證據，表明權重綁定優化了嵌入矩陣以進行輸出預測，從而妨礙了其在輸入表示中的作用。<lb/>這些結果有助於解釋為什麼權重綁定在大規模時可能會損害性能，並對訓練較小的LLM有影響，因為嵌入矩陣對總參數數量的貢獻相當可觀。
2603.26663.author: Antonio Lopardo et.al.
2603.26663.authors: Antonio Lopardo, Avyukth Harish, Catherine Arnett, Akshat Gupta
